meta.goal: Build github.com/Autocss-com/autocss = the refined, completed version of `starter`. Measuring stick: the finished app must look, behave, and work like `dhcp` (the working prototype), changing nothing except to improve to the D7460N Architecture. Separate repo so the two originals are never touched.

repos.autocss: this repo - best-of-both: starter's architecture, completed using dhcp as the target, every feature rewritten to D7460N.
repos.starter: the D7460N-correct rewrite of dhcp; CSS-driven visibility done, but incomplete. Architecture reference.
repos.dhcp: messy-but-complete working prototype; the FEATURE + LOOK reference (an MVP, a floor not a ceiling).

rules: CLAUDE.md in this repo is canonical and non-negotiable.
HTML+CSS do everything; JS is CRUD-only (API transport).

JS's single UI bridge is `oninput` on state-machine <input>s - fired by BOTH user action AND programmatically (initial load/restore = select the nav radio: set .checked=true + input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})) so the input's OWN oninput fires). NEVER click/onclick/change/addEventListener/dynamic import(). dispatchEvent is the SINGLE SANCTIONED exception: used ONLY to make a programmatically-selected nav radio fire its own oninput on load/restore (no real user event exists there); all real user interactions fire oninput natively - no dispatch.
No data-*/class/id; drive dirty/valid/enabled via CSS :has(:valid)/:has(:invalid)/:checked/:empty/:not(:empty)/aria-disabled.
JSON -> elements via toTagName() is allowed; no innerHTML string-building for presentation.
No utils/helpers/utility file names - name each module for what it DOES.
Semantic clarity beats cleverness; modern ES6+ baseline; stateless/idempotent; one API base.
Size: smaller is a guideline not a rule; D7460N compliance may need more code - if autocss ends up larger than dhcp, explain why.
meta.heuristic: Per feature (HTML + companion CSS + companion JS together, companion tracking mandatory): (1) what does it do; (2) is there a MORE MODERN native HTML/CSS way - if yes, STOP and ASK the user before changing markup/CSS, every time (identifying != applying); if no, keep unchanged; (3) will the universal 'one UI to rule them all' app ever need it - YES is the overwhelming default = KEEP & MODERNIZE. Dropping is a rare exception: only if truly redundant, and ALWAYS confirm with user first. Partial/half-built features: inspect, default to COMPLETE not drop.
meta.verify: Enabled env: network=Full (data hosts + Playwright CDN), browser=Playwright chromium via setup script. Tests = JS-logic node unit tests + static/compliance checks + Playwright DOM/behavioral/regression. Pixel-perfect bleeding-edge CSS fidelity needs a HUMAN eye (user does this manually).
meta.cadence: File-by-file, feature-by-feature. Stop and ask on ANY doubt. Check in before FIRST push and after each numbered build step. Test each feature + run regression as we go. Keep companions in lockstep.
meta.principle: USER PRIORITY (restated + elevated 2026-06-15): do the complex things everyone else over-engineers, but in SIMPLE, direct, declarative-first, browser-first, non-complex ways => easy, fast, small, cheap, interoperable. LESS complexity, NEVER more. Every change must REDUCE complexity, not add it; when in doubt the simpler/more-declarative path wins.
future_goals: POST-MVP interoperability demo: build reference instances embedding this ONE UI into each major CMS + framework (WordPress, Drupal, React, Vue, Angular, etc.) to prove it interoperates with the over-engineered/complex systems. ALL instances serve ONE shared instance of this UI from a CDN. The point: declarative-first/browser-first interoperates with everything AND is simpler/faster/smaller/cheaper.
Color-theme selection (next session): persist + restore exactly like nav and color-scheme (state-machine selection -> browser storage).

build_order: 1. Repo skeleton + PROGRESS.json + SessionStart hook + CLAUDE.md.
2. CSS base (starter set; reconcile dhcp concerns; inspect candidate-drops diag/stars/hero/_* and confirm with user before dropping).
3. index.html single universal scaffold (incl. dropdown-capable nav + its CSS).
5. Data shaping: schema(dhcp domain for now)/rules/ + concern-named modules (NEVER utils/helpers) - node tests.
7. CRUD UI forms + forms.css(de-data-*) + fallbacks.css + loading.css.
8. Wire dhcp datasets; full behavioral + regression pass in the enabled env.

_self.on_rotate: start progress/log-NNN.ndjson (next number), register it in shards[], update cursor.
_self.never: never edit past records; only append corrections (record type=lesson or note).
_self.resume: read PROGRESS.json first, then the newest shard, to restore context + why.

cursor.last: MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM Part 1, round 5 (2026-06-15, claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2): SIMPLIFY per user (Least Power, no session tracking). Deleted hydrateShell() + the isShellHydrated flag + 'Shell hydrated' log; inlined banner/nav-label/version injection as plain sequential calls in the SINGLE on-load function initializeOnInputLifecycle() (runs once from app.js; per-page data still loads via the nav radio's own oninput). Kept nav-label/banner/version text injection exactly as before. Renamed scheme->color-scheme everywhere (index.html name=color-scheme x3, color-scheme.css selectors+comments, oninput.js bindColorSchemeOnInput + selectors); storage key stays camelCase colorScheme. BROWSER-VERIFIED: API-down -> color-scheme persists/restores/round-trips; API-up -> nav labels inject + page data loads (10 rows, H1 Manage) + color-scheme 5/5. FUTURE NOTE: nav items will later be BUILT from JSON (like table cells) instead of a static skeleton. Round 4 (prior): BUGFIX (user-reported: choice not saving cross-session). ROOT CAUSE (reproduced + fixed in a REAL browser via Playwright/chromium): bindSchemeOnInput()+restoreColorScheme() ran AFTER `await hydrateShell()`, so a failed/slow data hydrate skipped them -> radios never bound to persistColorScheme, restore never ran; CSS color flip still worked (pure :has(:checked)) so it LOOKED fine but never persisted. FIX: moved both to run FIRST, BEFORE the await (color is pure UI, must not depend on the data API; scheme radios are static markup at parse time). BROWSER-VERIFIED both API-down (Light now persists+restores despite hydrate throwing) and API-up (5/5: Light saves+restores, System round-trips to OS-follow, no permanent override). Nav binding still gated behind hydrateShell (unchanged; nav needs data). Round 3 (prior): wired JS selection persistence + on-load restore into the EXISTING oninput.js init/storage (no new system). Added (mirroring the nav pair): bindSchemeOnInput() [persists each input[name=scheme].oninput value; NO lifecycle call, NO dispatchEvent], restoreColorScheme() [light/dark -> radio.checked=true, NO dispatch; absent/system -> nothing], getInitialColorScheme()/persistColorScheme() [read/write a colorScheme key BESIDE navigation in the autocss.app.v1 state via read/writePersistent]. Wired into initializeOnInputLifecycle() after the nav bind/trigger. JS sets only .checked, never styles; CSS reacts to :checked. node --check (ESM) passes. DEFERRED still: --txt/--bg/--accent->--fg/--bg consumer bridge; focus-indicator paint; BROWSER VERIFICATION. NOT yet browser-verified.

open_q: PORT-AS-IS is the active strategy (comply-later = THIS BUILD ONLY). Compliance fixes + PWA are SEPARATE future sessions - see SESSION-HANDOFF COMPLIANCE-DEBT LEDGER + FUTURE SESSIONS.

SAVE/DELETE DATA-MODEL DEFECT (HIGH-VALUE; own future session; recommended FIRST): Save succeeds (200) but writes to the WRONG LEVEL - POSTs a new top-level mockapi resource instead of editing record[0].items; Delete never hits the server. 3 causes: (1) read is nested (record[0].items) but write is flat (POST to collection root) - no path writes into record[0].items; (2) id-lookup bug querySelector('label > id') never matches the real <id-> cell (FACT #2) so Save always POSTs + Delete is DOM-only; (3) denormalize puts item field names at resource level. Affects ALL 12 endpoints. FIX = OPTION A (fix id selectors to 'label > id-', carry record[0]'s top-level id via a read accessor in oninput.js, splice item into record[0].items then PUT the whole resource) OR OPTION B (per-item backend routes). Full analysis: NDJSON 2026-06-10 lesson + SESSION-HANDOFF LEDGER. Old Q2 RESOLVED -> FIX (not keep-faithful).
